description: Qualified electricians for repairs, rewiring, installations, and emergency call-outs across the local area.

fault finding.advice: Reset the trip switch once — if it trips again, leave it off and avoid using the affected circuit until we can take a look.
fault finding.quote_notes: Simple fault traced to a single circuit is £90-110. Multiple circuits or an intermittent fault that takes time to find is £130-160. Replacing a faulty consumer unit (fuse box) part can push it to £180.

socket repair.advice: Avoid using the socket until it's been checked — a loose or damaged socket can be a fire risk.
socket repair.quote_notes: Replacing a single faulty socket is £60-80. Adding a new socket from an existing one is £100-120 depending on cable run.

lighting installation.advice: Make sure to switch off the circuit at the consumer unit if you've spotted any wires hanging.
lighting installation.quote_notes: Replacing an existing light fitting is £80-100. Installing a new light with cabling is £150-200. Multiple downlights in a ceiling is £200-250.

rewiring.advice: We recommend a free survey first — pricing depends a lot on the size of the property and how much access we have.
rewiring.quote_notes: Small flat or 2-bed terrace is typically £3,000-4,000. Standard 3-bed semi is £4,500-5,500. Large detached or homes needing replastering can reach £6,500.

EV charger installation.advice: Worth checking if you qualify for a government grant — we can guide you through that during the quote.
EV charger installation.quote_notes: Standard 7kW charger on an exterior wall close to the consumer unit is £800-950. Longer cable runs or trenching across a driveway can push it to £1,400.
